entries.definition:
247
occurs 10 times in the Qur’an.

حَاق ḥāqa [v. intrans.] to befall, to return upon, to overwhelm, to rebound (35:43) وَلَا يَحِيقُ الْمَكْرُ السَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِ evil plotting only rebounds on those who plot.
entries.definition_english:
247
occurs 10 times in the Qur’an.

حَاق ḥāqa [v. intrans.] to befall, to return upon, to overwhelm, to rebound (35:43) وَلَا يَحِيقُ الْمَكْرُ السَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِ evil plotting only rebounds on those who plot.

entries.definition:
h–y–y life, living, to live, to bring to life, to keep alive; livelihood; animal kingdom; fertility, rain; living quarters, district; to keep vigil, to occupy with activity, to celebrate the memory of; to greet, greeting; to be modest, to be shy, bashfulness; adder, serpent. Of this root, 15 forms occur 189 times in the Qur’an: حَيَّ ḥayya seven times; حَيَّا ḥayyā three times; حُيِّيَ ḥuyyiya once; أَحْيَا ʾaḥyā five times; يَسْتَحْيِي yastaḥyī nine times; حَيّ ḥayy 19 times; أَحْيَاء ʾaḥyāʾ five times; حَيَاة ḥayātun 76 times; حَيَوَان ḥayawān once; تَحِيَّة taḥiyyatun six times; اِسْتِحْيَاء ʾistiḥyāʾ once; مَحْيَا maḥyā twice; مُحْيِي muḥyī twice; يَحْيَى yaḥyā five times and حَيَّة ḥayyatun once.
248 ح/ي/ي

حَيَّ ḥayya a [an assimilated form of the verb حَيِيَ ḥayiya, intrans.] 1 to live (7:25) قَالَ فِيهَا تَحْيَوْنَ وَفِيهَا تَمُوتُونَ وَمِنْهَا تُخْرَجُونَ He said, ‘There you will live; there you will die; from there you will be brought out’ 2 to survive, to remain alive (8:42) لِيَهْلِكَ مَنْ هَلَكَ عَنْ بَيِّنَةٍ وَيَحْيَى مَنْ حَيَّ عَنْ بَيِّنَةٍ that he who perished might perish by a clear proof, and that he who survived might survive by a clear proof.

حَيَّا ḥayyā [v. II, trans.; pass. حُيِّيَ ḥuyyiya] to greet, to salute (58:8) وَإِذَا جَاؤُوكَ حَيَّوْكَ بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللَّهُ when they come to you they greet you with words God never used to greet you.

أَحْيَا ʾaḥyā [v. IV, trans.] 1 to bring to life, to give life to (45:26) قُلِ اللَّهُ يُحْيِيكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ say, ‘It is God who gives you life, then causes you to die’ 2 to restore to life (2:164) وَمَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مِنْ مَاءٍ فَأَحْيَا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا and in the water which God sends down from the sky to restore life to the earth after it had been lifeless 3 to help to live, to preserve someone’s life (16:97) مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً whosoever does right, whether male or female, and has faith—We will make them live a good life.

اِسْتَحْيَا ʾistaḥyā [v. X] I [intrans. with prep. مِن or particle أَنْ] to feel shy, to be embarrassed, to be ashamed (33:53) فَإِذَا طَعِمْتُمْ فَانْتَشِرُوا وَلَا مُسْتَأْنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ يُؤْذِي النَّبِيَّ فَيَسْتَحْيِي مِنْكُمْ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَسْتَحْيِي مِنَ الْحَقِّ then, when you have eaten, disperse, and do not stay on desiring a chat, for that used to inconvenience the Prophet, but he felt embarrassed [to ask] you [to go], but God is not embarrassed by the truth II [trans.] to spare from killing, to retrieve from, to save from death (28:4) يُذَبِّحُ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيِي نِسَاءَهُمْ slaughtering their sons and sparing the lives of their women.

حَيّ ḥayy [quasi-act. part., pl أَحْيَاء ʾaḥyāʾ] 1 living (21:30) وَجَعَلْنَا مِنَ الْمَاءِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ حَيٍّ and We fashioned from water every living thing 2 [اَلْحَيّ al-ḥayy an attribute of God], the Living (25:58) وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى الْحَيِّ put your trust in the Living [God] 3 living things (6:95) إِنَّ اللَّهَ فَالِقُ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوَى يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَمُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتِ مِنَ الْحَيِّ it is God who splits open the seed and the fruit-stone: He brings out the living from the dead and the dead from the living 4 [adverbially] alive (19:15) وَسَلَامٌ عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَ وُلِدَ وَيَوْمَ يَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ يُبْعَثُ حَيًّا peace be on him the day he was born, the day he dies, and the day he is raised alive.

h–y–y life, living, to live, to bring to life, to keep alive; livelihood; animal kingdom; fertility, rain; living quarters, district; to keep vigil, to occupy with activity, to celebrate the memory of; to greet, greeting; to be modest, to be shy, bashfulness; adder, serpent. Of this root, 15 forms occur 189 times in the Qur’an: حَيَّ ḥayya seven times; حَيَّا ḥayyā three times; حُيِّيَ ḥuyyiya once; أَحْيَا ʾaḥyā five times; يَسْتَحْيِي yastaḥyī nine times; حَيّ ḥayy 19 times; أَحْيَاء ʾaḥyāʾ five times; حَيَاة ḥayātun 76 times; حَيَوَان ḥayawān once; تَحِيَّة taḥiyyatun six times; اِسْتِحْيَاء ʾistiḥyāʾ once; مَحْيَا maḥyā twice; مُحْيِي muḥyī twice; يَحْيَى yaḥyā five times and حَيَّة ḥayyatun once.
248 ح/ي/ي

حَيَّ ḥayya a [an assimilated form of the verb حَيِيَ ḥayiya, intrans.] 1 to live (7:25) قَالَ فِيهَا تَحْيَوْنَ وَفِيهَا تَمُوتُونَ وَمِنْهَا تُخْرَجُونَ He said, ‘There you will live; there you will die; from there you will be brought out’ 2 to survive, to remain alive (8:42) لِيَهْلِكَ مَنْ هَلَكَ عَنْ بَيِّنَةٍ وَيَحْيَى مَنْ حَيَّ عَنْ بَيِّنَةٍ that he who perished might perish by a clear proof, and that he who survived might survive by a clear proof.

حَيَّا ḥayyā [v. II, trans.; pass. حُيِّيَ ḥuyyiya] to greet, to salute (58:8) وَإِذَا جَاؤُوكَ حَيَّوْكَ بِمَا لَمْ يُحَيِّكَ بِهِ اللَّهُ when they come to you they greet you with words God never used to greet you.

أَحْيَا ʾaḥyā [v. IV, trans.] 1 to bring to life, to give life to (45:26) قُلِ اللَّهُ يُحْيِيكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ say, ‘It is God who gives you life, then causes you to die’ 2 to restore to life (2:164) وَمَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مِنْ مَاءٍ فَأَحْيَا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا and in the water which God sends down from the sky to restore life to the earth after it had been lifeless 3 to help to live, to preserve someone’s life (16:97) مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً whosoever does right, whether male or female, and has faith—We will make them live a good life.

اِسْتَحْيَا ʾistaḥyā [v. X] I [intrans. with prep. مِن or particle أَنْ] to feel shy, to be embarrassed, to be ashamed (33:53) فَإِذَا طَعِمْتُمْ فَانْتَشِرُوا وَلَا مُسْتَأْنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكُمْ كَانَ يُؤْذِي النَّبِيَّ فَيَسْتَحْيِي مِنْكُمْ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَسْتَحْيِي مِنَ الْحَقِّ then, when you have eaten, disperse, and do not stay on desiring a chat, for that used to inconvenience the Prophet, but he felt embarrassed [to ask] you [to go], but God is not embarrassed by the truth II [trans.] to spare from killing, to retrieve from, to save from death (28:4) يُذَبِّحُ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيِي نِسَاءَهُمْ slaughtering their sons and sparing the lives of their women.

حَيّ ḥayy [quasi-act. part., pl أَحْيَاء ʾaḥyāʾ] 1 living (21:30) وَجَعَلْنَا مِنَ الْمَاءِ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ حَيٍّ and We fashioned from water every living thing 2 [اَلْحَيّ al-ḥayy an attribute of God], the Living (25:58) وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى الْحَيِّ put your trust in the Living [God] 3 living things (6:95) إِنَّ اللَّهَ فَالِقُ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوَى يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَمُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتِ مِنَ الْحَيِّ it is God who splits open the seed and the fruit-stone: He brings out the living from the dead and the dead from the living 4 [adverbially] alive (19:15) وَسَلَامٌ عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَ وُلِدَ وَيَوْمَ يَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ يُبْعَثُ حَيًّا peace be on him the day he was born, the day he dies, and the day he is raised alive.

entries.definition:
249
حَيَاة ḥayātun [n.] 1 state of being alive, life as opposed to death (67:2) الَّذِي خَلَقَ الْمَوْتَ وَالْحَيَاةَ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا who created death and life to test you [people and reveal] which of you is best in deeds 2 this life (as opposed to the next) (20:97) قَالَ فَاذْهَبْ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِي الْحَيَاةِ أَنْ تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوْعِدًا لَنْ تُخْلَفَهُ he said, ‘Go! It is for you, in [this] life, to say, “I shall not touch nor will I be touched [lit. No touching!]”, but you have an appointment that will not be broken’ 3 life span (17:75) إِذًا لَأَذَقْنَاكَ ضِعْفَ الْحَيَاةِ وَضِعْفَ الْمَمَاتِ then We should have made you taste double [the punishment] of life and double [the punishment] of death 4 living (16:97) مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً whosoever does right, whether male or female, and has faith—We shall make him live a good life 5 survival (2:179) وَلَكُمْ فِي الْقِصَاصِ حَيَاةٌ يَاأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ and in [the law of just] retribution there is survival for you, you who are endowed with insight; *(2:86) life of this world as opposed to the life Hereafter.

اَلْحَيَوَان ʾal-ḥayawān [n.] the real life, the perfect life (29:64) وَإِنَّ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ لَهِيَ الْحَيَوَانُ لَوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ the Hereafter is the [true] life, if only they knew.

تَحِيَّة taḥiyyatun [v. n./n.] greeting, saluting, salutation (33:44) تَحِيَّتُهُمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُ سَلَامٌ their greeting when they meet Him will be: ‘Peace.’

اِسْتِحْيَاء ʾistiḥyāʾ [v. n.] being shy, bashfulness (28:25) فَجَاءَتْهُ إِحْدَاهُمَا تَمْشِي عَلَى اسْتِحْيَاءٍ then one of the two came to him, walking shyly.

مَحْيَا maḥyā [n./v. n.] life/being alive (6:162) قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ say, ‘My prayers, all my acts of worship, my life/living and my death/dying are for God, Lord of all Being.’

مُحْيِي muḥyī [act. part.] one who restores to life (41:39) إِنَّ الَّذِي أَحْيَاهَا لَمُحْيِي الْمَوْتَى إِنَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ He who gives it life will certainly restore life to the dead. He is able to do everything.

يَحْيَى Yaḥyā [proper name] John the Baptist (Mat., III.1–12)

Zachariah, يَا زَكَرِيَّا إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَامٍ اسْمُهُ يَحْيَى لَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُ مِن قَبْلُ سَمِيًّا (19:7)
We bring you good news of a son whose name [will] be John—We
have chosen for him no namesake before. John the Baptist is the
cousin of Jesus, most of his story in the Qur’an appears in Sura 3.

حَيَّة hayyatun [n.] serpent, adder (20:20) فَأَلْقَاهَا فَإِذَا هِيَ حَيَّةٌ تَسْعَى
He threw it down and—lo and behold—it is a moving snake.

خ / khāʾ

الخاء ʾal-khāʾ the seventh letter of the alphabet, it represents a
voiceless uvular fricative sound.
entries.definition_english:
249
حَيَاة ḥayātun [n.] 1 state of being alive, life as opposed to death (67:2) الَّذِي خَلَقَ الْمَوْتَ وَالْحَيَاةَ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا who created death and life to test you [people and reveal] which of you is best in deeds 2 this life (as opposed to the next) (20:97) قَالَ فَاذْهَبْ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِي الْحَيَاةِ أَنْ تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوْعِدًا لَنْ تُخْلَفَهُ he said, ‘Go! No touching!]”, but you have an appointment that will not be broken’ 3 life span (17:75) إِذًا لَأَذَقْنَاكَ ضِعْفَ الْحَيَاةِ وَضِعْفَ الْمَمَاتِ then We should have made you taste double [the punishment] of life and double [the punishment] of death 4 living (16:97) مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً whosoever does right, whether male or female, and has faith—We shall make him live a good life 5 survival (2:179) وَلَكُمْ فِي الْقِصَاصِ حَيَاةٌ يَاأُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ and in [the law of just] retribution there is survival for you, you who are endowed with insight; *(2:86) life of this world as opposed to the life Hereafter.

تَحِيَّة taḥiyyatun [v. n./n.] greeting, saluting, salutation (33:44) تَحِيَّتُهُمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُ سَلَامٌ their greeting when they meet Him will be: ‘Peace.’

مَحْيَا maḥyā [n./v. n.] life/being alive (6:162) قُلْ إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ say, ‘My prayers, all my acts of worship, my life/living and my death/dying are for God, Lord of all Being.’

مُحْيِي muḥyī [act. part.] one who restores to life (41:39) إِنَّ الَّذِي أَحْيَاهَا لَمُحْيِي الْمَوْتَى إِنَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ He who gives it life will certainly restore life to the dead. He is able to do everything.

يَحْيَى Yaḥyā [proper name] John the Baptist (Mat., III.1–12)

Zachariah, يَا زَكَرِيَّا إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَامٍ اسْمُهُ يَحْيَى لَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُ مِن قَبْلُ سَمِيًّا (19:7)
We bring you good news of a son whose name [will] be John—We
have chosen for him no namesake before. John the Baptist is the
cousin of Jesus, most of his story in the Qur’an appears in Sura 3.

حَيَّة hayyatun [n.] serpent, adder (20:20) فَأَلْقَاهَا فَإِذَا هِيَ حَيَّةٌ تَسْعَى
He threw it down and—lo and behold—it is a moving snake.

خ / khāʾ

الخاء ʾal-khāʾ the seventh letter of the alphabet, it represents a
voiceless uvular fricative sound.
